title: Encyclia aspera
intro: Encyclia aspera, also known as the rough-encyclia, is a species of orchid native to Central and South America, characterized by its unique, rough-textured flowers. Traditionally, Encyclia aspera has been used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, respiratory problems, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties. The bioactive constituents of Encyclia aspera include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Encyclia aspera include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds.
taxonomy: Encyclia aspera is a species of orchid, classified within the genus Encyclia and the family Orchidaceae. The genus Encyclia is a group of orchids native to the Americas, typically characterized by their showy flowers. Encyclia aspera is further grouped into the subfamily Epidendroideae, which includes most terrestrial orchids. This classification system is based on evolutionary relationships and shared characteristics among the species. Encyclia aspera's classification reflects its unique characteristics and its position within the broader orchid family.
